description: Construction sequence, trade dependencies, interference resolution, prefabrication
refinery_construction_sequence: Phase 1: Site prep — survey, grading, temp power/lighting, laydown areas
Phase 2: Underground — piling, underground piping, ground grid, duct banks, conduit, foundations
Phase 3: Structural & equipment — steel erection, vessels, heavy equipment, transformers, control room
Phase 4: Bulk work — piping, cable tray, conduit, tubing runs, fireproofing
Phase 5: Termination — pipe welding, cable pulling, terminations, instrument mounting/wiring, pressure testing, heat tracing
Phase 6: Pre-commissioning — megger testing, switchgear checks, calibration, loop checks, motor bumps, flushing
Phase 7: Commissioning — energization, instrument air startup, DCS/SIS checkout, interlock testing, process introduction

structural: Steel erected for cable tray mounting
Platforms and grating installed for access
mechanical: HVAC operational in electrical rooms before energization
piping: Process connections complete and hydro tested before instrument tie-in
Pipe supports in place before routing conduit on pipe racks
Process taps installed and hydro tested
Piping flushing complete before inline instruments
mechanical: Equipment with instruments set and aligned

interference_resolution.priority_order: Process piping > structural steel > electrical trays > instrument trays > HVAC
process: Area coordinator calls meeting with affected foremen
Engineering provides disposition within 24-48 hrs (4 hrs for critical path)
Document all — often becomes change order backup
common_interferences: Cable tray blocked by piping routed differently than model
Instrument tubing through space now occupied by bracing
Valve handwheel conflicts with cable tray
Motor JB orientation wrong for conduit route

electrical: Conduit rack assemblies with pre-welded supports
Junction boxes pre-wired with terminal strips
prefabrication.benefit: Prefab achieves 3-4x field productivity. 8 hours in field = 2 hours in shop (controlled environment, proper tooling, bench-height, no permits).
prefabrication.rule: Only prefab from APPROVED FOR CONSTRUCTION (AFC) drawings. Prefabbing from IFR drawings = rework.
